message:
fix(backend): auth gaps, race conditions, panic guards, selector injection (#6996-#7004) (#7027)

* fix(agent): atomic token write, detach ctx for error writes, defer heartbeat close, mixed-mode done msg

- #6996: Use atomic write (tmp+rename) in saveTokenUsage to prevent
  corruption from concurrent goroutines.
- #6997: Use context.Background() when writing provider error responses
  so the error reaches the client even if the mission context expired.
- #7001: Defer close(heartbeatDone) so the heartbeat goroutine is
  always stopped, even if StreamChatWithProgress panics.
- #6999: Send TypeResult with Done:true after TypeStreamEnd in
  handleMixedModeChat so the UI clears the spinner and unlocks input.

Fixes #6996, #6997, #6999, #7001

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix(agent): ctx.Done in prediction interval, atomic running flag, auth on 12 HTTP handlers

- #6998: Add ctx.Done() case to interval-wait select in PredictionWorker
  runLoop, mirroring the initial-delay select.
- #7002: Replace bool running field with atomic.Bool and use
  CompareAndSwap to eliminate the unlocked window between check and
  runAnalysis in both runLoop and TriggerAnalysis.
- #7000: Add validateToken auth check to 12 HTTP handlers that were
  missing it (namespaces, deployments, replicasets, statefulsets,
  daemonsets, cronjobs, ingresses, networkpolicies, services,
  configmaps, serviceaccounts, jobs).

Fixes #6998, #7000, #7002

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix(api): validate tail param and sanitize workload name in deploy-logs

- #7003: Clamp negative tail query parameter to default (8) to prevent
  panic from out-of-bounds slice access.
- #7004: Validate workload name against Kubernetes label value pattern
  before interpolating into label selector to prevent selector injection.

Fixes #7003, #7004

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6,6 +6,7 @@ import (
 	"fmt"
 	"log/slog"
 	"math"
+	"regexp"
 	"sort"
 	"strconv"
 	"strings"
@@ -366,6 +367,10 @@ type ClusterGroup struct {
 const allHealthyClustersGroupName = "all-healthy-clusters"
 
 // In-memory cluster group store (persisted via frontend localStorage; backend is source of truth for labels)
+// validLabelValue matches Kubernetes label values: alphanumeric, '-', '_', '.'
+// up to 63 characters. Used to prevent label selector injection (#7004).
+var validLabelValue = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9]([a-zA-Z0-9._-]{0,61}[a-zA-Z0-9])?$`)
+
 var (
 	clusterGroups   = make(map[string]ClusterGroup)
 	clusterGroupsMu sync.RWMutex
@@ -1190,7 +1195,12 @@ func (h *WorkloadHandlers) GetDeployLogs(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
 	cluster := c.Params("cluster")
 	namespace := c.Params("namespace")
 	name := c.Params("name")
-	tailLines := c.QueryInt("tail", 8)
+	const defaultTailLines = 8
+	tailLines := c.QueryInt("tail", defaultTailLines)
+	// Clamp to a safe range to prevent panic from negative slice indices (#7003).
+	if tailLines <= 0 {
+		tailLines = defaultTailLines
+	}
 
 	client, err := h.k8sClient.GetClient(cluster)
 	if err != nil {
@@ -1200,6 +1210,13 @@ func (h *WorkloadHandlers) GetDeployLogs(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
 	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(c.Context(), workloadDeployLogsTimeout)
 	defer cancel()
 
+	// Validate workload name to prevent label selector injection (#7004).
+	// A crafted name like "foo,env=prod" would expand to "app=foo,env=prod"
+	// and match pods from unrelated workloads.
+	if !validLabelValue.MatchString(name) {
+		return c.Status(400).JSON(fiber.Map{"error": "invalid workload name"})
+	}
+
 	// Try label selector first: app=<name>
 	pods, err := client.CoreV1().Pods(namespace).List(ctx, metav1.ListOptions{
 		LabelSelector: fmt.Sprintf("app=%s", name),
